title: melicope magnifolia
intro: Melicope magnifolia, also known as the White Milu or White Neem, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and limonoids. The health benefits of Melicope magnifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, as well as its antiseptic and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, Melicope magnifolia is often used as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
